items.title: XRP jumps 4% as traders watch 'triangle breakout' toward $1.35
items.description: XRP pushed higher over 24 hours and tested short-term resistance, but the larger chart still needs a clean break above the $1.24-$1.28 supply zone to confirm a stronger reversal.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin ETFs post a fifth straight day of inflows in a first since April
items.description: The run has pulled in roughly $727 million, the most sustained stretch of buying since the record outflows of June.

items.title: UK Parliament begins inquiry into banking chokepoint for crypto businesses
items.description: The U.K.’s Crypto and Digital Assets All-Party Parliamentary Group (APPG) is focused on banks that refused crypto firms accounts or introduced restrictions on crypto transactions.

items.title: Bitcoin hits a two-week high near $65,500 as the chip trade turns back into a tailwind
items.description: Asian semiconductor shares rebounded hard, a five-day ETF inflow streak passed $600 million, and oil pulled back on Middle East diplomacy.

items.title: Inside Cardano's 'Van Rossum' hard fork, and what it means for users
items.description: The Van Rossem hard fork moved Cardano to version 11 on Saturday. And for the first time, Cardano's community voted in the upgrade, not the company that built it.

items.title: Exodus to cut 25% of global workforce in payments shift
items.description: This restructuring is part of a strategy to build a full-stack payments platform following the company's acquisitions of Monavate and Baanx.

items.title: AI compute stocks bounce as Hut 8, IREN land billions in new contracts
items.description: The deals sparked a rebound in AI compute stocks after investors questioned demand for new data center capacity.

items.title: Tom Lee's Bitmine slowed ether purchases as it bought back $86 million in stock
items.description: The company added just 7,430 ETH, worth about $14 million, last week as it nears its goal to corner 5% of Ethereum's supply.

items.title: UK parliamentary group probes banking barriers for crypto firms
items.description: A UK parliamentary group launched an inquiry into banking restrictions on crypto firms and consumers, including their impact on investment and competition.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs post five-day inflow streak, longest since May
items.description: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $227 million in inflows as BTC climbed above $65,000, extending their longest winning streak since early May.

items.title: Base’s 1:1-backed tokenized equities launch ‘imminent,’ Pollak says
items.description: The Coinbase-backed Ethereum layer-2 is preparing to expand its financial offerings as it pivots away from its earlier social-first strategy.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Grayscale files S-1 for first US Worldcoin ETF
items.description: The filing expands Grayscale’s growing lineup of crypto-related exchange-traded products outside of Bitcoin and Ether.

items.title: Celsius co-founders Leon, Goldstein to pay FTC over $6M
items.description: The settlements add to former Celsius CEO Alex Mashinsky’s $10 million FTC settlement in April.

items.title: Binance & OKX users face $1,900 fines in Vietnam, Coinbase in China? Asia Express
items.description: Retail users in Vietnam who trade on offshore crypto exchanges like Binance or OKX face heavy penalties. Network School controversy in Malaysia over Israeli citizen ban.

items.title: London Stock Exchange eyes overnight trading launch in 2027: FT
items.description: The move comes as traditional exchanges face growing competition from crypto markets and tokenized equity platforms that offer around-the-clock trading.

items.title: U.S. strikes Iran and Houthis threaten Saudi Arabia shipping as mediators push 10-day ceasefire
items.description: The latest cycle of strikes comes amid reports that regional mediators have presented Washington and Tehran with a proposal for a 10-day ceasefire.

items.title: Jamie Dimon says markets underestimate risks and he wouldn't buy stocks or Treasurys at current prices
items.description: Comments from JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon contrast with investors' recent willingness to look past wars, tariffs and other shocks.

items.title: General Motors is set to report earnings before the bell. Here's what Wall Street expects
items.description: Wall Street analysts expect General Motors to report adjusted earnings per share of $3.20 and revenue of $47.01 billion.

items.title: Trump imposing 50% tariffs on certain Canadian goods over alleged trade discrimination
items.description: Canada's relationship with the U.S. has frayed amid Trump's tariff-heavy agenda and vocal displeasure with the nations' trilateral trade pact with Mexico.

items.title: Boeing and Airbus prepare for next narrow-body battle – but airlines aren’t pushing for new jets yet
items.description: Boeing and Airbus are preparing their next narrow-body jets, but airlines and investors remain focused on production, deliveries and current aircraft.

items.title: Trump administration's head of AI safety agency resigns after 3 months on job
items.description: Arvind Raman, the director of National Institute of Standards and Technology, will serve as acting director of CAISI, according to a spokesperson

items.title: Abu Dhabi approves $6.2 billion gas project to boost UAE supply
items.description: Abu Dhabi's National Oil Company will invest $6.2 billion to develop the Umm Shaif Gas Cap to expand natural gas production and liquefied natural gas exports.

items.title: Samsung Electronics shares rise as robotics move highlights push into physical AI
items.description: Samsung Electronics shares rose as the company set up a robotics division in a push into physical AI.

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AQriP4CmlJs! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: China contemplates tit-for-tat export controls against U.S. on AI technologies: report
items.description: It could be a direct response to U.S. steps, it could be diplomatic posturing but competition between the U.S. and China for AI supremacy is hotting up.

items.title: Montana home of Kelly Clarkson’s late ex-husband Brandon Blackstock listed for $2.9 million
items.description: The sprawling Montana lodge where Kelly Clarkson’s late ex-husband moved to after they split for sale for $2.9 million—a year after the talent manager died following a battle with cancer.

items.title: Oil prices could surpass $120 per barrel if disruptions in Strait of Hormuz don’t ease, says Goldman Sachs
items.description: Analysts at the investment bank, led by Daan Struyven, see oil prices averaging $100 a barrel next year if traffic continues to be affected through the waterway.

items.title: Jamie Dimon says he wouldn’t buy Treasurys. ‘I don’t understand the upside.’
items.description: Jamie Dimon — long considered a potential candidate to be treasury secretary, and the person in charge of one of the nation’s primary dealers of Treasury securities — says he wouldn’t buy American debt.

items.title: Your healthcare could now cost $185,500 in retirement — and that doesn’t include long-term care
items.description: Rising prices for care and managing chronic conditions helped drive up the forecast.

items.title: U.S. hits Canada with stiff new tariffs, escalating trade tensions
items.description: The Trump administration on Monday said it would slap 50% tariffs on some goods imported from Canada, in an effort to end what it sees as discriminatory trade practices by Ottawa against the U.S. automobile, dairy and alcohol industries.

items.title: I racked up $35,000 in credit-card debt. Should I file for bankruptcy?
items.description: “I’m also considering working with a credit-counseling agency or enrolling in a hardship program.”

items.title: Why are gasoline prices rising faster than oil prices? Blame it on the ‘crack.’
items.description: The spread between the cost of barrels of gasoline and crude oil — known as the “crack spread” — has widened sharply.

items.title: How an AI-powered observability programme can transform operations | FT Tech

2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Thames Water lenders to offer ‘golden share’ to UK government
items.description: Creditors plan improved offer in bid to avoid nationalisation by new Burnham government

items.title: Who is in Andy Burnham’s new cabinet?
items.description: UK prime minister announces top team, with many experienced faces returning to government

items.title: UK wage growth continues to cool while unemployment rate steady
items.description: Bank of England will probably hold rates again in July amid mixed labour market signals

items.title: Continuity and change in Burnham’s new cabinet
items.description: Chancellor from Labour’s ‘old right’ is experienced but unlikely to meaningfully diverge from previous set up

items.title: Mitie agrees £3.1bn takeover by rival outsourcer OCS
items.description: Facilities management group is latest London-listed company to agree a deal

items.title: Indian asset manager’s debut revives hopes for local IPO market
items.description: Shares of SBI Funds Management jump nearly 9% in country’s largest listing so far this year

items.title: Brazil faces unforgiving economics after election
items.description: Whoever wins the presidential race will have to face up to the need for fiscal consolidation

items.title: Burnham move to eliminate VAT on electricity bills sparks funding row
items.description: Government claim that measure would be funded by scrapping digital ID scheme disputed by former minister

items.title: Why States Are Making It Harder to Save Money With Solar Panels
items.description: The changes are costing homeowners money. But they also reflect a changing grid — and the need for home batteries.

items.title: Trump to Impose 50% Tariff on Many Canadian Goods
items.description: The administration will use an untested legal provision to put significant duties on Canadian exports, reigniting a clash with one of America’s biggest trading partners.

items.title: Andy Burnham, New UK Prime Minister, Names John Healey As Top Finance Official
items.description: As chancellor, John Healey, who quit as defense secretary in the former government, will fill one of the most consequential posts, overseeing fiscal and economic policy.

items.title: Judge Temporarily Pauses Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
items.description: The delay is intended to give the court time to consider a lawsuit that argues the $111 billion merger violates antitrust laws.

items.title: Trump Administration Sought Phone Records of Times Journalists and Their Relatives
items.description: The government is aggressively seeking the confidential sources of Times reporters who wrote about the president’s new Air Force One. The Times has asked a judge to quash the subpoenas.

items.title: How Google’s A.I. Search Is Imperiling the Open Web
items.description: As Google incorporates more artificial intelligence into search, people are spending more time on Google. Some website operators are crying foul.

items.title: Soaring Egg Prices Are Hitting China Hard
items.description: China consumes more eggs per capita than almost every other country, so a recent spike in costs is touching a nerve.

items.title: American A.I. Giants Like Alphabet Face Fresh Tests
items.description: Rapid advancements in Chinese artificial intelligence models raise more questions about costly technology spending, as Google’s parent prepares to report earnings.
